note: Park et al. 2023, Generative Agents (UIST '23, arXiv:2304.03442v2). LLM-simulation of human behavior: 25 gpt-3.5-turbo agents in a Sims-like sandbox (Smallville). Controlled evaluation is a WITHIN-SUBJECTS, SINGLE-FACTOR ablation (5 conditions: full architecture + 3 nested/cumulative ablations + human crowdworker baseline), dependent variable = human-judged BELIEVABILITY (rankings -> TrueSkill), analyzed with Kruskal-Wallis ('a non-parametric alternative to the one-way ANOVA') + Dunn post-hoc + Holm-Bonferroni; Cohen's d=8.16. End-to-end eval = descriptive stats over 2 game days. The words 'factorial'/'full-factorial' appear 0 times in the paper; the design is one-way (single factor with 5 levels), the OPPOSITE of a full-factorial crossing of >=2 factors.

C090.citation_role: park2023generative listed among social-science / LLM-simulation domain applications
C090.evidence: Generative Agents is an LLM-simulation of human social behavior (emergent info diffusion, relationship formation, coordination among 25 agents) and is legitimately part of the social-science / LLM-simulation domain literature. Its inclusion in a background domain-list mention is defensible.
C090.source_loc: park Sec 1 (Intro), Sec 3.4 (Emergent Social Behaviors), Sec 7 (End-to-End Evaluation)
C090.note: Non-quantitative background domain-list mention; no specific numeric/methodological claim about park is made here. Recorded ACCURATE (consistent with team-lead determination); park's categorization as social-science-relevant LLM-simulation holds.

C091.manuscript_text: Automated-social-science pipelines~\citep{manning2024automated} and LLM-simulation studies~\citep{aher2023turing,park2023generative} validate with statistical tests over full-factorial designs.
C091.evidence_accurate_part: The 'LLM-simulation study' label is ACCURATE for park (25 LLM agents simulating human behavior), and the 'validate with statistical tests' part is also supported: park uses Kruskal-Wallis + Dunn post-hoc + Holm-Bonferroni + Cohen's d.
C091.evidence_mismatch_part: The descriptor 'full-factorial designs' MISCHARACTERIZES park. Park's controlled evaluation is a within-subjects, SINGLE-FACTOR (condition) design with 5 levels, NOT a crossing of >=2 factors at all level combinations. The 3 architecture ablations are strict nested subsets (none -> observation -> observation+planning -> observation+planning+reflection), i.e., a cumulative ablation staircase (4 of the 2^3=8 possible component combinations), plus a human crowdworker baseline. The authors themselves analyze it with 'the Kruskal-Wallis test, a non-parametric alternative to the ONE-WAY ANOVA' -- explicitly a one-way (single-factor) analysis, the opposite of full-factorial. The DV is human-judged believability, not a factorial causal outcome. The word 'factorial' does not appear anywhere in the paper (grep 0 hits).
quotes: "The study followed a within-subjects design, where 100 participants compared interview responses generated by four different agent architectures and a human-authored condition for the same agent." (Sec 6.1, L1169-1171)
"we applied the Kruskal-Wallis test [56], a non-parametric alternative to the one-way ANOVA, to the raw rank data. We then performed the Dunn post-hoc test [98]..." (Sec 6.4, L1252-1254)
"Our dependent variable is the believability of the behavior, a central dependent variable in prior work on agents (e.g., [10])." (Sec 6.1, L1133-1135)

Ablation ladder: 'no observation, no reflection, no planning ...; a no reflection, no planning architecture ...; and a no reflections architecture ...' (Sec 6.2, L1184-1189) -- nested subsets, not a factorial crossing.
C091.reasoning: The phrase 'full-factorial design' is a specific experimental-design term (all combinations of >=2 crossed factors). Park runs a one-way ablation validated with one-way non-parametric tests. Calling this 'full-factorial' is not minor wording slack but a categorically different design type, so the park attribution to 'full-factorial designs' overstates/miscategorizes its methodology. The 'full-factorial' descriptor better fits manning2024automated (automated-social-science pipeline); it should not be extended to park.
C091.recommended_fix: Drop park2023generative from the 'full-factorial designs' grouping, or soften the clause. E.g., attribute 'full-factorial designs' to manning2024automated only, and describe park (and aher) as validating simulated behavior with statistical tests over ablation / controlled comparisons -- not full-factorial designs. Minimal edit: '...LLM-simulation studies~\citep{aher2023turing,park2023generative} validate simulated behavior with statistical tests (e.g., ablation comparisons and non-parametric significance tests).'

reverse_findings.fact: Park's controlled evaluation is a within-subjects SINGLE-FACTOR ablation with 5 conditions (full + 3 nested ablations + human crowdworker), analyzed by Kruskal-Wallis, which the authors describe as 'a non-parametric alternative to the one-way ANOVA', plus Dunn post-hoc + Holm-Bonferroni.
reverse_findings.mismatch: Manuscript L448 groups park under 'validate with statistical tests over full-factorial designs'. A one-way ablation analyzed by a one-way ANOVA analogue is NOT a full-factorial design (which requires crossing >=2 factors over all level combinations). 'factorial' appears 0 times in park.

reverse_findings.fact: Park's dependent variable is human-judged BELIEVABILITY (participant rankings converted to TrueSkill ratings), and its 'human' comparator is a crowdworker roleplay baseline explicitly stated NOT to represent maximal/expert human performance.
reverse_findings.mismatch: The clause implies hypothesis-testing/validation over crossed experimental factors against ground truth. Park validates the believability of an interactive-simulacra demo via subjective human rankings, not a factorial causal test against real-human behavioral/survey data.

reverse_findings.fact: Park is fundamentally a UIST/HCI interactive-systems demonstration of generative agents (memory/reflection/planning architecture), with a two-part evaluation (controlled ablation + descriptive end-to-end emergent-behavior measurements over 2 game days).
reverse_findings.mismatch: Bundling park with automated-social-science pipelines (manning2024automated) as a statistical-validation-over-factorial-designs exemplar overstates park's role; park is not a hypothesis-testing pipeline over a factorial parameter space. End-to-end results (info diffusion 4%->32% / 4%->52%, network density 0.167->0.74, 5/12 party attendance) are descriptive, not inferential-factorial.
